target: data/wins.json -> WIN-001 (id="001"), detail_evidence field. Insert two new paragraphs between the existing "The dome's formula exposes the relabeling." paragraph and the existing "Tesla's location destroys the dome interpretation." paragraph. No other WIN-001 field is modified.
patch_instructions:
In data/wins.json, locate the WIN with id="001". In its detail_evidence field, find the ‘The dome's formula exposes the relabeling.’ paragraph. This paragraph ends with the verbatim sentence "the disc_thickness parameter that produces 11.78 Hz matches Earth's diameter (12,742 km) rather than any dome parameter." Immediately after that sentence — i.e., replacing only the "

" separator that currently precedes the "Tesla's location destroys the dome interpretation." paragraph — insert exactly the following sequence:

+ [PARAGRAPH A supplied in replacement_html, block 1] +

+ [PARAGRAPH B supplied in replacement_html, block 2] +

Do not modify the preceding "dome formula exposes the relabeling" paragraph. Do not modify the subsequent "Tesla's location destroys the dome interpretation" paragraph. Do not touch detail_verdict_text, detail_claim, detail_extra, tldr_evidence, or tldr_verdict — the TLDRs do not invoke the v_a derivation or the cross-WIN speed swap, so no TLDR change is warranted here.
replacement_html:
The second derivation is the same formula in disguise. The dome publishes both the wins.html shorthand f = c/(2 × disc_thickness) and, on its live-power page, a second form f = v_a / (2 × r_disc) with v_a = 1.574c. A fair-minded defender will point to that second form as the "real" dome-parameter derivation, since both v_a and r_disc sit in the dome's constants box. The defense fails on the numbers. The aetheric wave speed v_a = 1.574c is numerically ≈ πc/2 = 1.5708c (ratio 1.0020), the standard phase velocity of the fundamental mode of a spherical cavity resonator of Earth's radius — a textbook ionospheric-waveguide result, not a dome prediction. The disc radius r_disc = 20,015 km equals π × R_earth = 20,015.09 km to four decimals — Earth's half-circumference under the polar azimuthal-equidistant projection used by every globe-to-flat mapping since Snyder (1987). Substitute both into f = v_a / (2 × r_disc) and the live-page formula collapses to (πc/2)/(2 × πR_earth) = c/(4 R_earth) = c/(2 D_earth) — the wins.html formula in different variable names. The closing tell is the ratio itself: v_a/c = 1.574 = 20,015 / 12,717 = r_disc / disc_thickness. The dome's two "different" derivations are the same derivation; v_a was chosen precisely so the live-page numbers reproduce the wins.html numbers. The live-power form does not rescue the claim — it re-dresses the same Earth-sphere arithmetic.

Same circuit, two incompatible wave speeds. The dome's live-power page asserts that WIN-001 (Tesla 11.78 Hz) and WIN-029 (Schumann 7.83 Hz) sit on the same aetheric circuit, with cross-correlation r = 0.999 between Tesla's frequency and the Schumann fundamental. A single medium, however, has a single propagation speed. The two derivations do not: WIN-001 uses v_a = 1.574c in f = v_a / (2 × r_disc), while WIN-029 uses vacuum c in H_firmament = c / (4f), yielding H = 299,792 / (4 × 7.83) ≈ 9,572 km (claimed 5.3% match to the model's H = 9,086 km). Apply v_a consistently across the "same circuit" and WIN-029's ceiling shifts to v_a / (4 × 7.83) ≈ 15,066 km — 66% higher than the dome's published H, destroying WIN-029's match to the firmament apex. Apply c consistently to WIN-001 via f = c / (2 × r_disc) and the Tesla frequency drops to c / (2 × 20,015) = 7.49 Hz — far from 11.78 Hz. The dome preserves both WINs only by selecting c for one and v_a for the other, on the same circuit, without reconciliation. This is the diagnostic signature of per-WIN parameter fitting, not unified physical modeling.

holes_addressed: H1 (ISS-1250): Missing v_a/(2 × r_disc) preemption. Our prior text only rebutted the wins.html f = c/(2D) formula and never engaged the live-power f = v_a/(2 × r_disc) formula, leaving a defender free to say "you attacked the wrong formula." Paragraph A closes this loop by showing (a) the dome publishes both derivations; (b) v_a = 1.574c ≈ πc/2 is the spherical-cavity phase velocity; (c) r_disc = 20,015 km = π × R_earth to four decimals; (d) the ratio v_a/c = 1.574 = 20,015/12,717 = r_disc/disc_thickness is an identity by construction, so both formulas are numerically one derivation in two suits of clothes.

H2 (ISS-1251): Cross-WIN c-vs-v_a speed swap between WIN-001 and WIN-029. The dome claims a single aetheric circuit (r = 0.999) but uses v_a = 1.574c for WIN-001 and vacuum c for WIN-029 without reconciliation. Paragraph B quantifies the incompatibility both directions: applying v_a to WIN-029 shifts H to ≈15,066 km (66% high); applying c to WIN-001 via f = c/(2 × r_disc) shifts the Tesla frequency to 7.49 Hz (38% low). The dome keeps both matches only by selecting speed per-WIN — the diagnostic signature of ad hoc fitting.
new_evidence_added: Arithmetic identity π × R_earth = 20,015.09 km (verified: python 20015.086796020572) = r_disc to four decimals.
Phase-velocity identity v_a = 1.574c ≈ πc/2 = 1.5708c (ratio 1.0020) — standard textbook result for spherical cavity resonator fundamental mode.
Ratio identity v_a/c = 1.574 = r_disc/disc_thickness = 20,015/12,717 = 1.5739. Numerical coincidence-by-construction, not a dome-physics derivation.
Cross-WIN applied-v_a check: v_a / (4 × 7.83) = 15,066 km vs dome H = 9,086 km (+66%).
Cross-WIN applied-c check: c / (2 × 20,015) = 7.49 Hz vs claimed 11.78 Hz (−38%).
Reference to Snyder (1987) for polar azimuthal-equidistant projection provenance of the πR_earth relabeling.
Cross-reference to the dome's live-power page declaration of the "same aetheric circuit" relationship (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 147).

anticipated_objections.objection: The two formulas having a numerical identity is a property of any two formulas that compute the same physical quantity; of course they collapse to each other. That does not prove relabeling.
anticipated_objections.response: The identity holds only because v_a was chosen to equal (π/2) × c and r_disc was chosen to equal π × R_earth. Neither constant is derived from a dome first principle — the dome does not publish a calculation for either. They match Earth's sphere values to four decimals because they were set to those values. A genuine dome-physics derivation of v_a and r_disc would produce numbers that happen to reduce to Earth's values only if the dome corresponded to Earth's spherical geometry, which it does not claim to.

anticipated_objections.objection: The "same aetheric circuit" claim does not require identical wave speeds — different modes can have different phase velocities on the same physical medium.
anticipated_objections.response: That is true in a dispersive waveguide, but the dome offers no dispersion relation and no mode index — v_a and c are used as plain propagation speeds in WIN-001 and WIN-029 respectively, not as dispersion-derived phase velocities for distinct modes. And the r = 0.999 "correlation" the dome cites is a correlation between f_Schumann and 1/T_Tesla, a cross-frequency match that explicitly asserts a shared timing relation. If the modes had different speeds on the same medium, a dispersion law would be the first thing published; it is not.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome uses v_a in derivations where the aetheric circuit dominates and c where the vacuum propagation term dominates — this is not inconsistency, it is appropriate per-regime selection.
anticipated_objections.response: Then the model owes a derivation of which regime applies where, with a predictive criterion independent of the confirming number. None is published. In practice the selection is made post hoc — c where c matches the observation, v_a where v_a matches — with no formula that forecasts the regime before the answer is known. That is operational ad hoc fitting regardless of the motivating narrative.

kernel_of_truth: v_a = 1.574c is a real and interesting number: it is within 0.2% of πc/2, which is the well-known fundamental-mode phase velocity of a spherical cavity resonator with Earth's radius, and appears in ionospheric waveguide physics from Watson (1919) onward. The dome correctly identifies the number. And r_disc = 20,015 km is an exact match to π × R_earth — also correct. What the dome does not get right is the inference: matching πc/2 and πR_earth to four decimals is not evidence of a disc — it is evidence that Earth's spherical-cavity constants have been imported under disc labels. The sphere did the work; the disc kept the credit.
cross_references: WIN-029 (Schumann Resonance Requires Hard Conductive Ceiling) — directly targeted by paragraph B.
WIN-002 (Schumann 7.83 Hz) — already discussed in WIN-001 detail_extra; cross-WIN speed swap is independent of the selective-damping argument.
Section 2.1 (which already leads with the pole-floor argument about c/(4H)) — paragraph B can be read as the spot-check complement to Section 2.1's general argument.
unaddressed_holes_note: This EXP addresses only H1 (ISS-1250) and H2 (ISS-1251) from the WIN-001.c3 review. Three other holes remain for separate EXPs or the decider: the triplicated "selective damping signal" paragraph in detail_extra (hole #1, major — pure editorial deduplication, not an analytical hole); the πR_earth-to-r_disc explicit closing sentence (hole #4, moderate — largely subsumed by paragraph A's explicit πR_earth = 20015.09 km statement, but the curmudgeon's recommendation to add the sentence in the same location where v_a = πc/2 is mentioned may still warrant a micro-edit); and the generic IGRF/NASA post-hoc boilerplate in detail_verdict_text (hole #5, minor). The decider should route the detail_extra dedup as a non-analyst patch and consider whether hole #4 is fully covered by this EXP.
newsworthy: Dome's two "independent" 11.78 Hz derivations are one derivation: v_a/c = r_disc/disc_thickness by construction.
notes_for_decider: Insertion point is explicit in patch_instructions. The two new paragraphs are supplied verbatim in the fields paragraph_A_h1 and paragraph_B_h2 (and concatenated in replacement_html for integration tooling). No TLDR change is recommended because the existing tldr_evidence already captures the "Earth's diameter not dome parameter" punchline at layperson level and neither new paragraph changes the verdict; extending the TLDR to cover the v_a/r_disc identity would exceed the 2–3 sentence budget without a proportional readability gain. detail_verdict_text and detail_extra do not need to be touched here — they engage separate rhetorical surfaces (post-hoc standard and selective damping respectively). If the decider prefers a TLDR touch-up, one safe edit is to append a single sentence to tldr_evidence: "The live-power page's alternate formula is the same identity in different variable names." But that is optional, not part of the curmudgeon's recommendation.
